meta.title: US Recession by Q4 2026
meta.question: Will the US economy enter a recession (NBER call or 2+ consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth) before Dec 31 2026?
meta.resolution: NBER recession dating committee announcement OR BEA published estimates showing 2+ consecutive negative real GDP quarters with end-quarter at or before Q4 2026

priorJustifications.H1: Modal but with regime conditioning. Curve has un-inverted (partial relief signal); Sahm rule has not triggered (requires +0.5pp 3m-avg unemployment off the low); unemployment 4.3% still low absolute. NY Fed yield-curve recession-probability models at this stage of un-inversion typically print 25-35%. Net: regime-conditional 12-mo recession probability ~50%, with H1 capturing 'no NBER call by Dec 31 2026' over the ~9-month horizon.
priorJustifications.H2: Mild recession is the modal recession scenario if one occurs — tariff drag + labor cooling could produce shallow contraction without systemic shock.
priorJustifications.H3: Moderate recession requires a real shock (oil, banking, geopolitics). Possible given Hormuz situation but not modal.
priorJustifications.H4: Severe recession requires multiple compounding shocks; tail risk only.

meta.calibrationFlags: 7 of 17 indicators VALIDATED at full empirical-backtest standard from FRED authoritative data; 10 indicators PENDING_DATA_INGESTION (HY OAS, ISM PMI, BEA real PCE, Conference Board CCI, FDIC bank failures, etc.). Per-indicator status in topic.governance.calibrationReport.
meta.lensSetReason: Initial lens set during topic creation per skills/news-scan.md mapping

issues: Unfalsifiable hypotheses: H1, H2, H3, H4

blockers: NON-UNIFORM PRIORS without justification — posteriors are {'H1': 0.5, 'H2': 0.28, 'H3': 0.15, 'H4': 0.07} but posteriorHistory has no initial note explaining the asymmetry. Add a posteriorHistory entry documenting why.
warnings: No anti-indicators defined — every hypothesis needs at least one indicator that would REDUCE its probability
COVERAGE ASYMMETRY: H4 has 11 positive indicator(s) but no negative ones — it can only gain probability, never lose
COVERAGE ASYMMETRY: H3 has 11 positive indicator(s) but no negative ones — it can only gain probability, never lose
COVERAGE ASYMMETRY: H1 has 11 negative indicator(s) but no positive ones — it can only lose probability, never gain
INDISTINGUISHABLE: H3 and H4 share 11/11 indicators (overlap 100%) with 0 directional discriminators — consider merging or adding an indicator that moves them in opposite directions

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier1_critical.desc: Sahm rule fires: 3-month moving avg unemployment rate rises >=0.5pp from prior 12-month low. Source: BLS monthly Employment Situation. Phase-3.5 EMPIRICAL CALIBRATION (REV2 of fixture): LRs derived from FRED UNRATE-derived trigger events 1953-2024 cross-referenced with NBER recession-depth classifications. Excludes pre-1953 (no Sahm-derivable data). H2 raised from 0.50 to 0.80 after fixture refinement (3/3 in-window H2 recessions had Sahm fire vs prior count 2/4 that included pre-data 1948).

Headline NFP (not revision) prints negative for 2 CONSECUTIVE months. Source: BLS Employment Situation. Phase-3.5 EMPIRICAL (FRED PAYEMS): empirical H1 ~0.029 (0 H1 false positives across 78 years 1948-2025). LR set to 0.05 — slightly above empirical to keep max/min ratio under phantom_precision lint threshold of 20; well within Beta(1,1) small-N uncertainty band.

4-week moving average of initial unemployment claims breaches 400k. Source: DOL weekly. Phase-3.5 EMPIRICAL (FRED ICSA): empirical H1 ~0.015 (0 H1 false positives 1967-2025); H2 ~0.50 (1 of 2 in-window H2 recessions breached 400k). LR set to H1=0.05 to keep max/min ratio under phantom_precision threshold of 20; H2=0.50 matches empirical.

tier2_strong.desc: 10y-3m Treasury spread re-inverts (negative) after un-inversion, sustained 5 trading days. Source: FRED T10Y3M. Phase-3.5 EMPIRICAL: declared H1 (0.30) was 5x over empirical (~0.06) — yield curve inversions are rare in calendar terms (only 6 distinct events 1982-2025); 2 false positives (1998, 2022-24). H4 declared (0.65) under empirical (0.75) by 0.10. Revised to match.
ISM Manufacturing PMI prints <45 for 2 consecutive months. Source: ISM monthly.
ISM Services PMI prints <50 for 2 consecutive months. Source: ISM monthly.
ICE BofA US High Yield OAS breaches 800bp (deeper than 600bp threshold to mark genuine systemic stress; currently ~330bp), sustained 10 trading days. Source: FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2.

Brent crude breaches $120/bbl spot, sustained 30 calendar days. Source: ICE Brent front-month. Phase-3.5 EMPIRICAL (FRED POILBREUSDM): empirical H1 ~0.044 (2 false positives in window: 2011-12, 2022); H2 sample size n=1 (1990-91 H2 didn't fire); H4 1/2 (2008 fired, 2020 didn't — COVID oil crash). Declared H1 was 7x over empirical.
At least one large-bank failure (>=$50B in assets) OR Fed announces emergency liquidity facility >$200B notional, occurring within 2026. Source: FDIC + Federal Reserve press releases.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Senior Loan Officer Survey shows >50% net tightening of C&I lending standards. Source: Fed quarterly SLOOS. Phase-3.5 EMPIRICAL (FRED DRTSCILM): empirical H1 ~0.044 (1 false positive 2023_Q3 borderline); 2007-09 H4 fired 4 quarters; 2001 H1_TECHNICAL fired. Pre-1990 recessions (1973, 1980, 1981) not in DRTSCILM window. Declared H1 was 8x over empirical.
Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index <55 (current ~95). LR trimmed in REV4 — fired in 2011 and 2022 without recession. Source: Conference Board monthly.

anti_indicators.desc: 3-month moving NFP avg >250k for any 3-month window after July 2026. Source: BLS.

4-week moving avg initial claims drops below 200k. Source: DOL weekly. Phase-3.5 EMPIRICAL: declared H1 LR (0.85) was massively over-stated — empirical FRED ICSA shows 4wk MA dropped <200k in only ~2 calendar years out of 57 in window (2018, 2019, briefly 2022). The declared treated 'recent default state' as base rate. Empirical H1 ~0.05; recession Hs effectively 0 (no historical recession had claims-MA <200k).
ICE BofA US High Yield OAS sustained <300bp for 60 trading days at any point in 2026 (financial-conditions axis anti, NOT labor). Source: FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2.

Sahm rule fires THEN un-fires (3-month MA falls back below 0.5pp threshold sustained 3 months) within 2026 — distinguishes near-miss H1 recovery from H2 mild-recession entry. LR magnitude restrained (small historical sample, mostly 2024). Source: BLS.

After any tier1 recession indicator (Sahm/payrolls-neg-2mo/claims-400k) has fired in 2026, BEA real Personal Consumption Expenditure growth prints >=2.0% YoY for 2 consecutive quarters (post-trigger anti — consumer-spending decouples from labor signal, replicates 2024 false-positive pattern). Source: BEA Personal Income & Outlays.

ICE BofA US High Yield OAS peaks below 600bp through Q4 2026 AND no large-bank failure (>=$50B assets) in 2026 (joint condition — eliminates the systemic-stress pathway H4 requires). Source: FRED + FDIC.

actorModel.description: Recession in 2026 emerges from interaction of: Fed reaction function, tariff-driven inflation, labor market state, oil supply shock risk, and credit conditions. NBER dating committee is the formal arbiter; BEA quarterly GDP prints are the concrete observable.

fomc.name: Federal Open Market Committee
fomc.role: Sets policy rate; reaction function determines whether labor cooling becomes recession or soft landing.
fomc.decisionStyle: Data-dependent, consensus-driven, slow to surprise markets.
biases: Weight employment leg of dual mandate when U-3 rises
filters: NFP, U-3, core PCE, financial conditions
overrides: Systemic banking event forces emergency easing regardless of inflation

trump_administration.name: Trump Administration (Treasury + USTR)
trump_administration.role: Tariff policy creates inflation pulse; fiscal policy adds demand. Regime that conditions Fed's space to ease.
trump_administration.decisionStyle: Willing to absorb short-term volatility for structural goals; tariff escalations possible without warning.
biases: Pressure on Fed for cuts via public statements
View tariffs as revenue not inflation
overrides: Visible recession + Republican electoral risk forces stimulus pivot

households.name: US Consumer Households
households.role: 70% of GDP. Consumer spending durability is the single biggest H1-vs-H2-3 differentiator.
households.decisionStyle: Adapt slowly; spending often holds even as confidence collapses (excess savings buffer).
biases: Lag spending response to job loss
overrides: Mass layoff event triggers immediate retrenchment regardless of confidence

credit_markets.name: Credit Markets (HY OAS, IG spreads, banks)
credit_markets.role: Forward-looking aggregator of recession probability. Credit stress amplifies any downturn.
credit_markets.decisionStyle: Bayesian; fast-moving; prone to overshoot in stress.
biases: Liquidity-driven false signals (2011, 2015-16, 2022 spread blowouts without recession)
filters: HY OAS, SLOOS tightening, bank failures, Fed liquidity facilities
overrides: Fed emergency facility caps spreads regardless of fundamentals

oil_market.name: Oil Supply / OPEC+ / Geopolitics
oil_market.role: Brent above $120 sustained 30 days adds stagflation risk, raising recession probability via consumer + Fed channels.
oil_market.decisionStyle: OPEC+ seeks $80-100 floor; Hormuz / Russia / Venezuela are tail risks.
biases: Underestimate demand destruction at high prices
Saudi-Russia coordination breaks down under stress
overrides: Hormuz closure forces emergency demand response

nber_dating_committee.name: NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee
nber_dating_committee.role: Sole arbiter of recession dating. Resolves topic via on-record announcement.
nber_dating_committee.decisionStyle: Slow, deliberate, ex-post. Typically dates ~6-18 months after peak.
filters: Real personal income ex-transfers, payrolls, real PCE, industrial production

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — Trump tariff threats are RHETORIC until federal register; FOMC dot plots are DATA; FOMC votes are EVENT.
MULTI-CHANNEL CONFIRMATION — A single labor print is noise; require >=2 of {labor, manufacturing, services, credit} breaking before treating recession probability as substantively shifted.
OIL SHOCK SEPARATION — Brent>$120 routes to H3 (moderate, demand destruction) not H2 (mild) — matches historical stagflation patterns.
NBER LAG — NBER calls usually arrive 6-18 months after peak. Don't expect resolution before Q2 2027 even if recession started Q1 2026.
CREDIT-SPREAD FALSE POSITIVES — HY OAS blowouts in 2011/2015-16/2022 did not produce recession. Require bank-event confirmation before strong H4 update.

bls_employment_situation.label: BLS Employment Situation (NFP, U-3, hours, earnings)
bls_employment_situation.note: Drives Sahm rule, payrolls indicators, U-3 thresholds. Single release fires multiple indicators — REV3 binds via causal_event_id=bls_dol_labor.

dol_initial_claims.label: Initial unemployment claims, 4-week moving average
dol_initial_claims.note: Flow-side labor signal. Antis tightened to <200k in REV4.

bea_gdp_quarterly.label: BEA real GDP, advance/second/third estimate
bea_gdp_quarterly.note: Resolution-class for technical recession; REV4 removed direct BEA-print indicator.

bea_pce.label: BEA Personal Income & Outlays (core PCE YoY, real PCE growth)

ism_manufacturing_pmi.label: ISM Manufacturing Report on Business
ism_manufacturing_pmi.note: Tier2 H3 indicator. Shares ism_pmi_print causal_event_id with services.

ism_services_pmi.label: ISM Services Report on Business
ism_services_pmi.note: Tier2 H3 indicator. Same monthly survey shop as manufacturing PMI.

ice_bofa_hy_oas.label: ICE BofA US High Yield Option-Adjusted Spread (FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2)
ice_bofa_hy_oas.note: Drives credit_spread_blowout (>800bp, H4) AND anti_credit_spreads_compressed (<300bp, H1). REV4 shares causal_event_id=systemic_credit_stress_2026.

fdic_bank_failures.label: FDIC Failed Bank List + Fed emergency liquidity facility announcements
fdic_bank_failures.note: t2_systemic_bank_event indicator. >=$50B asset failure OR Fed facility >$200B.

ice_brent_frontmonth.label: ICE Brent crude oil front-month spot price
ice_brent_frontmonth.note: Brent>$120 sustained 30 days fires t2_brent_120_to_h3 (rerouted from H2 in REV3).

fred_yield_curve_t10y3m.label: 10-year minus 3-month Treasury spread (FRED T10Y3M)
fred_yield_curve_t10y3m.note: Re-inversion sustained 5 trading days fires t2_yield_curve_re_inverts.

fed_sloos.label: Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey on Bank Lending Practices
fed_sloos.note: >50% net tightening of C&I lending fires t3_slos_tightening.

conference_board_cci.label: Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index
conference_board_cci.note: CCI<55 fires t3_consumer_confidence_55. LR trimmed in REV4 (false-positive 2011/2022).

nber_dating_committee.label: NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee announcements
nber_dating_committee.note: Resolution arbiter. Typically dates 6-18 months after peak.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF technical recession, THEN unemployment peaks at 5.0%-5.3% by Q4 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: US unemployment rate maximum in Oct-Dec 2026 in 5.0%-5.3% range per BLS

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Directionally captures recession, but 5.0-5.3% peak smooths over the cascade risk in a real downturn where unemployment typically overshoots.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF technical recession, THEN unemployment breaks above 5.0% by Q4 2026 and continues rising into Q1 2027 as claims-to-payroll feedback loop engages, with 6m change exceeding Sahm trigger by Dec 2026.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: 5.0-5.3% peak is on the high edge; with Fed pre-committed to cuts in a technical recession, labor market absorption typically keeps peak lower. Overstates breakdown.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Unemployment peak 5.0-5.3% in a technical recession is too shallow. RED's prior is 5.5-6.0% given labor-hoarding unwind and automation displacement. Shift band up.
Unemployment 5.0-5.3% peak is sharp.
